Title: Mechanisms of glacial-to-future atmospheric CO2 effects on plant immunity

Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sub-ambient CO2 (200 ppm), ambient CO2 (400 ppm), Hpa infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript measured in eight-leaf shoots; reduced after Hpa inoculation at 48 and 72 hpi under both ambient and sub-ambient CO2
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reduced after Hpa inoculation at 48 and 72 hpi
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Pathogen-induced repression of CAT2 is associated with enhanced accumulation of photorespiration-derived intracellular H2O2 and increased Hpa resistance at sub-ambient CO2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAT2 transcript abundance decreased after Hpa inoculation at 48 and 72 hpi. 2. CAT2 is proposed to scavenge GOX-derived H2O2 during photorespiration. 3. Repression of CAT2 during infection likely permits greater intracellular ROS accumulation at sub-ambient CO2, contributing to SA-independent resistance against Hpa.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS defense signaling against biotrophic pathogen infection
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: UPLC-Q-TOF metabolomics; targeted SA/JA UPLC-Q-TOF MS
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course of CAT2 expression after mock and Hpa inoculation; ROS staining (DCFH-DA); pathogen resistance phenotyping
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced CAT2 likely increases oxidative stress risk while enhancing defense
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CAT2 was not genetically manipulated in this study; role inferred from expression profiling and known scavenging function.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes glycolate to glyoxylate with concomitant production of H2O2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies photorespiration-derived H2O2 that promotes pathogen resistance signaling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for photorespiration-derived ROS contribution to immunity in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling contributing to resistance against Hyaloperonospora arabidopsidis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sub-ambient CO2 (200 ppm) and Hpa infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Measured in eight-leaf plants; transcript reduced in promoter insertion mutant gox1-2 by 42.6%
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional effect assessed in eight-leaf leaves during Hpa infection under sub-ambient CO2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced GOX1 expression abolished sub-ambient CO2-induced resistance to Hpa
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gox1-2 is a promoter insertion knock-down mutant with 42.6% lower GOX1 expression. 2. gox1-2 plants retained normal growth at sub-ambient CO2. 3. Unlike wild type, gox1-2 failed to express sub-ambient CO2-induced resistance against Hpa, supporting a crucial role for GOX-derived ROS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter insertion mutant analysis; RT-qPCR of GOX1 expression; Hpa resistance phenotyping; DCFH-DA intracellular ROS staining
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Photorespiratory GOX activity may be leveraged to enhance broad-spectrum immunity under low CO2-like conditions, but alterations must consider oxidative stress and growth effects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating GOX/photorespiration could alter redox balance and growth; strong loss of photorespiration is deleterious at ambient CO2
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single knock-down mutant used because stronger photorespiratory loss-of-function mutants show severe growth defects or lethality at ambient CO2.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Peroxisomal hydroxy-acid oxidation linked to photorespiratory ROS production
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxy acid substrate(s), likely glycolate-related hydroxy acids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes hydroxy acid substrate(s) with production of H2O2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies intracellular ROS linked to pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling contributing to Hpa resistance under sub-ambient CO2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sub-ambient CO2 (200 ppm) and Hpa infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Measured in eight-leaf plants; transcript reduced in promoter insertion mutant haox1-2 by 75.4%
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional effect assessed in eight-leaf leaves during Hpa infection under sub-ambient CO2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced HAOX1 expression abolished sub-ambient CO2-induced resistance to Hpa
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. haox1-2 is a promoter insertion knock-down mutant with 75.4% lower HAOX1 expression. 2. haox1-2 plants showed wild-type growth at sub-ambient CO2. 3. haox1-2 failed to express sub-ambient CO2-induced resistance against Hpa, indicating a requirement for hydroxy-acid oxidase-associated ROS production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter insertion mutant analysis; RT-qPCR of HAOX1 expression; Hpa resistance phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May serve as a target to modulate photorespiration-linked defense ROS, though substrate specificity and oxidative costs require caution.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely affects redox homeostasis; stronger perturbation of photorespiration may impair growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper groups HAOX1 with GOX-related photorespiratory ROS functions, but detailed catalytic assignment is less explicit than for GOX1.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Pathway-dependent hormonal control gene used as JA biosynthesis determinant
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Required for jasmonic acid biosynthesis-dependent defense activation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Elevated CO2 (1200 ppm) and Plectosphaerella cucumerina infection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential for elevated CO2-induced resistance to P. cucumerina because aos1-1 mutants failed to express the resistance phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 allene oxide synthase family
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because mutant analysis established necessity of intact JA biosynthesis for metabolically relevant defense outcome under elevated CO2.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Elevated CO2 increased JA accumulation in wild type. 2. aos1-1 mutants did not show elevated CO2-induced resistance to P. cucumerina. 3. Intact JA biosynthesis is therefore critical for the elevated CO2 defense phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports JA-responsive defense including induction/priming of VSP2-associated responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping under elevated CO2; lesion measurement after P. cucumerina infection; hormone profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing JA biosynthetic capacity could improve necrotroph resistance under future elevated CO2 environments.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense hormone promoting resistance to necrotrophic pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for elevated CO2-enhanced resistance to P. cucumerina

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Elevated CO2 (1200 ppm) and Plectosphaerella cucumerina infection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential for elevated CO2-induced resistance to P. cucumerina because jar1-1 mutants failed to express the resistance phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase family
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used to show requirement for JA sensitivity/signaling rather than biosynthesis alone.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss impairs defense signaling against necrotrophs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Elevated CO2 primed JA-inducible gene expression. 2. jar1-1 mutants failed to show elevated CO2-induced resistance to P. cucumerina. 3. JA perception/signaling is therefore critical for the elevated CO2 defense phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for JA-inducible defense responses including VSP2 induction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping under elevated CO2; lesion measurement after P. cucumerina infection
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be considered for tuning JA responsiveness to improve elevated CO2-associated necrotroph resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for elevated CO2-enhanced resistance to P. cucumerina

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Elevated CO2 or sub-ambient CO2 and Hpa infection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes partially, but is not solely responsible, to elevated CO2-induced Hpa resistance; not critical for sub-ambient CO2-induced Hpa resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutant analysis indicates SA pathway contribution is partial at elevated CO2 and dispensable for sub-ambient CO2-induced Hpa resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss increases susceptibility to biotrophic pathogen
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Elevated CO2 increased SA accumulation and primed SA-inducible PR1 expression. 2. sid2-1 retained significant elevated CO2-induced Hpa resistance, showing SA is only part of the mechanism. 3. sid2-1 also retained sub-ambient CO2-induced Hpa resistance, indicating SA signaling is not critical there.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports SA-responsive defense including PR1 induction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping under altered CO2; Hpa colonization assays; hormone profiling; PR1 expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SA pathway enhancement alone is insufficient to explain or fully engineer CO2-dependent Hpa resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense hormone promoting resistance to biotrophic pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Partially contributes to elevated CO2-induced Hpa resistance but is dispensable for sub-ambient CO2-induced Hpa resistance

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Required for salicylic acid signal transduction
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Elevated CO2 or sub-ambient CO2 and Hpa infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in SA-dependent transcriptional defense network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes partially, but is not solely responsible, to elevated CO2-induced Hpa resistance; not critical for sub-ambient CO2-induced Hpa resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutant analysis indicates SA response contributes but cannot fully account for elevated CO2-dependent Hpa resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss increases susceptibility to biotrophic pathogen
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Elevated CO2 primed SA-inducible PR1 expression. 2. npr1-1 retained significant elevated CO2-induced Hpa resistance, indicating SA signaling is only partially responsible. 3. npr1-1 also retained sub-ambient CO2-induced Hpa resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for full activation of SA-inducible genes such as PR1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping under altered CO2; Hpa colonization assays; PR1 expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: NPR1-centered engineering alone may not reproduce full elevated CO2-associated resistance to Hpa.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PR1 and other SA-responsive defense genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Partially contributes to elevated CO2-induced Hpa resistance but is dispensable for sub-ambient CO2-induced Hpa resistance
